steps.title: Overview: Specialized Designs
steps.description: The print generation tour covered the common kit contract. This tour shows how model-specific designs plug into it: Corsi-Rosenthal frames, donut filter adapters, and curated static references loaded from public assets.

steps.title: Design Presets
steps.description: Design presets are the user-facing catalog. Each preset carries model data, recommended fan and filter counts, source and license metadata, and defaults used when the design is selected.

steps.title: Corsi Model Factory
steps.description: The Corsi model factory chooses the topology and derives face roles, fan count, filter count, frame dimensions, and cassette sizing from the active layout.

steps.title: Corsi Kit Factory
steps.description: The Corsi kit generator builds rails, corner blocks, fan cassettes, sealed face parts, panel seals, glue keys, and connectors. It returns the same printable kit shape as the generated Nukit path.

steps.title: Corsi Split Rails
steps.description: Split rail groups describe long rails that may need multiple printable segments. The split joint type controls whether scarf keys or pocketed connectors are used.

steps.title: Donut Model Factory
steps.description: The donut model derives adapter dimensions from fan size and filter hole size. This is a separate design vocabulary from rectangular panel boxes.

steps.title: Donut Kit Factory
steps.description: The donut kit returns three parts: adapter, guard, and cap. This model has no large rectangular panel split, so its generator is much smaller than Corsi.

steps.title: Static Reference Catalog
steps.description: Static references wrap curated STL projects with file lists, plate previews, assembled previews, and attribution. They are shown in the same selector as generated models.

steps.title: Static Asset Loader
steps.description: Static STL assets load asynchronously from public URLs and are converted into Three.js meshes. The loader also chooses a print orientation for plate previews.

steps.title: Quick Check
steps.text: What lets Corsi, donut, and generated Nukit print designs share one export button?
answers: They all return a printable kit or static reference path through the print design routing
They all use the same STL files
They all use laser SVG as the export format
steps.explanation: `createPrintDesignKit` in `src/printDesignKit.ts` starts at line 17 and routes model-specific generators behind one call shape.

steps.title: Summary
steps.description: Specialized designs add their own model vocabulary, then return shared printable artifacts. The next tour switches to rendering and shows how generated models and static STL references become visible previews.
